items.name: Speaker Intro Slide
items.description: Speaker introduction — avatar + name + role + bio + links. Drop it early to set credibility.
items.snippet:
<SpeakerIntroSlide
  chapter="Speaker"
  avatar="/img/avatar.png"
  name="Jordan Rivera"
  role="Developer Advocate"
  bio="Builds tools so that writing software with AI feels like engineering, not improvisation."
  links={[{ label: "example.dev", href: "https://example.dev" }]}
/>

items.name: Code Scrolly Slide
items.description: Explanation cards on one side, code on the other; each step advances the active card and refocuses the code to the lines it describes. Scrollycoding, adapted to a stepped deck.
items.snippet:
<CodeScrollySlide
  chapter="Code"
  title="How review reads a change"
  lang="ts"
  code={source}
  steps={[
    { focus: [2, 3], title: "Load the spec", body: "No spec means flag and stop early." },
    { focus: [4, 4], title: "Load the plan", body: "The plan is the contract to judge against." },
    { focus: [5, 6], title: "Judge the diff", body: "Spec, plan, and diff decide the verdict." },
  ]}
/>
items.motion: step-driven via hidden data-step markers; active card highlights and code focus moves per advance; reduced motion drops the dim blur and card transition

items.name: Code Slideshow Slide
items.description: Step through code snapshots that swap with a fade, one caption per snapshot and a dot progress row. The before/during/after refactor slide.
items.snippet:
<CodeSlideshowSlide
  chapter="Code"
  title="Refactor, live"
  lang="ts"
  snapshots={[
    { code: "function add(a, b) {\n  return a + b;\n}", caption: "Start: a plain function." },
    { code: "const add = (a, b) => a + b;", caption: "Arrow + implicit return." },
    { code: "const add = (a: number, b: number): number => a + b;", caption: "Typed." },
  ]}
/>
items.motion: step-driven via hidden data-step markers; snapshot fade on advance (generic seam; a Code Hike token morph needs its DOM renderer); reduced motion disables the fade

items.name: Code Spotlight Slide
items.description: One code block; each step spotlights a different line range (the rest dim and soften) with a short note. Arrow keys walk the focuses via the engine step system.
items.snippet:
<CodeSpotlightSlide
  chapter="Code"
  title="A highlight hook, one beat at a time"
  lang="tsx"
  code={source}
  steps={[
    { focus: [1, 2], note: "State holds the result." },
    { focus: [3, 7], note: "Resolve the async highlight in an effect." },
    { focus: [6, 6], note: "Cancel on unmount so stale results never land." },
  ]}
/>
items.motion: step-driven via hidden data-step markers; per-line dim/blur transition on focus change; reduced motion drops the blur

items.name: Concept Slide
items.description: Eyebrow label + big serif title + optional subtitle. Use for concept defines (What is X?).
items.snippet: What is Spec-Driven Development?} />

items.name: Image Caption Slide
items.description: Title + large screenshot + optional caption. The workhorse of every recap section.
items.snippet:
<ImageCaptionSlide
  chapter="Benefits"
  title="Easy navigation between specs"
  image="/img/nav.png"
  alt="Sidebar"
  caption="Every feature has its spec, plan, and tasks — connected in the sidebar."
/>

items.name: Image Text Split Slide
items.description: Half image, half copy. Use for product detail beats or storytelling pairs.
items.snippet:
Rendered markdown with anchors to sub-documents.

} />

items.name: Quote Portrait Slide
items.description: Testimonial with a face — big quote + portrait (photo or initials) + name/role + optional logo. Social proof, vs. the text-only quote.
items.snippet:
<QuotePortraitSlide
  chapter="Proof"
  quote="It makes them look good in the room — audiences react really well."
  name="Jordan Rivera"
  role="Staff Engineer"
/>

items.name: Quote Slide
items.description: Pull quote with attribution. Use for evidence, a stakeholder voice, or a memorable framing.
items.snippet:
<QuoteSlide
  chapter="Quote"
  quote={<>Code without a spec is just a prompt that gets forgotten.</>}
  attribution="Ada Lovelace"
  role="Engineer"
  source="Notes on the Analytical Engine"
/>

items.name: Recap Slide
items.description: Closing recap of a section or the whole talk. A check-marker list anchors what the audience just heard.
items.snippet: What we saw today} items={[ { title: "Vibe coding fails at scale", sub: "the bug ships anyway" }, { title: "Spec-driven development", sub: "the spec before the code" }, { title: "An open standard", sub: "portable across tools" }, ]} />

items.name: Demo Cue Slide
items.description: Cue card for a live demo — accent badge + h1 + optional subtitle. Drops the speaker out of the deck and into the demo.
items.snippet:
<DemoCueSlide
  chapter="Live Demo"
  cue="Live Demo"
  iconName="terminal"
  title="Back to the spec we created"
  subtitle="We'll create it, plan it, and review it — live."
/>

items.name: Bento Grid Slide
items.description: Bento grid of mixed tiles (stat / text / quote), each spanning 1–2 cols/rows. The 'everything at a glance' dashboard beat.
items.snippet:
<BentoGridSlide
  chapter="Recap"
  title="The quarter, at a glance"
  tiles={[
    { value: "412", title: "PRs shipped", tone: "accent", colSpan: 2 },
    { value: "24m", title: "Median review", tone: "green" },
    { value: "1.4", title: "Reverts / 100", tone: "red" },
    { title: "What changed", body: "Specs made review a step, not a scramble.", colSpan: 2 },
  ]}
/>

items.name: Big Statement Slide
items.description: One huge line — the manifesto / 'one big word' / punchline beat. Centered, minimal, with one accent phrase.
items.snippet:
<BigStatementSlide
  chapter="Thesis"
  eyebrow="The point"
  sub="Steal the best ideas. Make it yours."
>
  The best workflow is the one you build yourself.
</BigStatementSlide>

items.name: Comparison Table Slide
items.description: Feature matrix (rows × columns) with check/x/partial or short-text cells and an optional highlighted winning column. The 'X vs Y' slide.
items.snippet:
<ComparisonTableSlide
  chapter="Compare"
  title="Why spec-driven wins"
  columns={["Vibe coding", "Spec + plan", "Reviewed"]}
  highlight={2}
  rows={[
    { label: "Structure", cells: [false, true, true] },
    { label: "Handoff survives", cells: [false, "partial", true] },
    { label: "Review built in", cells: [false, false, true] },
  ]}
/>

items.name: Full Bleed Slide
items.description: Edge-to-edge image with a text lockup over a scrim — the cinematic opener / chapter cover. Degrades to a gradient if the image is missing.
items.snippet:
<FullBleedSlide
  chapter="Chapter"
  eyebrow="Part II"
  title="Ship faster, break less"
  subtitle="How spec-driven delivery changed the numbers."
  anchor="bottom-left"
/>

items.name: Timeline Slide
items.description: Horizontal timeline / roadmap — dated beats (when + title + body) on a connecting line. Use for history or now·next·later.
items.snippet:
<TimelineSlide
  chapter="Roadmap"
  title="How the workflow evolved"
  items={[
    { when: "v0", title: "Vibe coding", body: "No structure, lost context", color: "red" },
    { when: "v1", title: "Spec + plan", body: "Two files you own", color: "yellow" },
    { when: "v2", title: "Reviewed", body: "Review built into the loop", color: "green" },
    { when: "next", title: "Your workflow", body: "Steal the best, own it", color: "accent" },
  ]}
/>

items.name: Agenda Slide
items.description: Numbered list of chapters with an optional 'you are here' highlight. Sets the audience's expectations.
items.snippet:
<AgendaSlide
  chapter="Agenda"
  current={1}
  items={[
    { num: "01", title: "The problem", sub: "vibe coding fails at scale" },
    { num: "02", title: "Spec-driven development", sub: "the cycle" },
    { num: "03", title: "An open standard" },
    { num: "04", title: "Live demo" },
  ]}
/>

items.name: Cold Open Slide
items.description: Cold open — background + eyebrow only; the title is held back to land on the first click.
items.snippet: The Big Idea} subtitle="Hold the title back, then land it." />

items.name: Cover Slide
items.description: Deck opener — eyebrow, big display-font title, subtitle, optional faded background image.
items.snippet: Ship Faster, Break Less} subtitle="A practical guide to spec-driven delivery." />

items.name: Section Divider Slide
items.description: Visual chapter break — large number + section name. Lets the audience reset between acts.
items.snippet: Spec-Driven Development} subtitle="The cycle that frames the rest of the talk." />

items.name: Stat Row Slide
items.description: Row of 3–4 metric cards with accent edge bars, big display numbers, and an optional secondary line. Use to frame results or scale at a glance.
items.snippet:
<StatRowSlide
  chapter="Results"
  title="The quarter in three figures"
  stats={[
    { value: "412", label: "PRs shipped", sub: "+38% throughput", color: "accent" },
    { value: "24m", label: "Median review", sub: "down from 71m", color: "green" },
    { value: "1.4", label: "Reverts / 100", sub: "from 6.2", color: "red" },
  ]}
/>
